blockingIssues.description: The feature closes VAL-CROSS-003 by pointing at `.factory/validation/mission-verification/feature-owned-proof-followup.json`, but that artifact declares `featureId: "mission-cross-flow-feature-owned-proof-followup"` and mission metadata marks that combined feature as superseded with no fulfills claims. This narrower pause-continuation feature therefore still lacks its own feature-owned checked-in proof artifact.

The cited `runtime-controls.json` pause evidence still relies on isolated paused renderer-state injection for the critical paused-chat step, so it does not satisfy the requirement for new same-session Mission-GUI-TEST proof of user pause -> continue via normal chat.

The daemon-recovery feature also closes by reusing the shared `feature-owned-proof-followup.json` bundle rather than adding daemon-feature-owned proof for VAL-CROSS-004, so the required narrower ownership and deliverable are still missing.

Existing Mission-GUI-TEST evidence proves daemon failure -> retry -> `mission_paused`, but the continue-via-normal-chat portion still depends on focused tests plus injected paused-state validation rather than new same-session Electron proof owned by this feature.

Commit `a039b57` adds a VAL-CROSS-005 owner bundle, but the bundle overstates what `runtime-controls.json` proves: the pending-kill capture is a synthetic pre-async check and the paused-chat screenshot is reused from a generic paused continuation assertion, not a real same-session kill-worker completion path.

The kill-worker feature remains a proof-ownership remap without fresh same-session Mission-GUI-TEST runtime proof that a real kill-worker action on the cited Mission session produced `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` while keeping the same Mission inspectable and resumable.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify `mission-verification-worker` further: a newly-added owner file that only remaps older artifacts does not satisfy a same-session E2E proof requirement unless the underlying substantive proof is also newly feature-owned and not dependent on injected-state-only validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The VAL-CROSS-005 follow-up added a new owner bundle and still marked `followedProcedure: true`, but scrutiny found the substantive kill-worker evidence remained older artifacts plus injected renderer-state checks rather than fresh same-session Mission-GUI-TEST proof.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document a bookkeeping convention for proof-only features that are satisfied by already-checked-in artifacts: either record a worker session plus handoff/transcript anyway, or encode an explicit artifact-only completion path in mission metadata so later scrutiny has a traceable audit chain.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The completed proof features `mission-cross-flow-validator-completion-proof` and `mission-cross-flow-restart-reselection-proof` passed review from commit/artifact evidence alone because `completedWorkerSessionId` remained null and no matching handoff/transcript existed, weakening traceability even though the underlying proof mapping was acceptable.

rejectedObservations.observation: Both passing proof-only follow-up reviews reported the same missing transcript/handoff convention gap for completed features backed by already-checked-in artifacts.
